Petition For Order Authorizing Trustee To Borrow Money, To Execute Promissory Note, And To Execute Deed Of Trust In Order To Give Security
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Alfred Cavagnaro (Trustee). Lawrence Siracusa for Ebe Sapone (Conservatee). Matthew Mraule for Lucilla Sapone.

Mr. Lachona stated that First Republic Bank is the only bank that will lend to a Trustee but that they require a Guarantor which leaves Ebe or his Client, that Pacific Private Bank will loan to a Trustee, that it will not request a personal guarantee, that it is not a recourse loan, that the interest rate is between 7.75 to 8.25 with a 3% fee for a 2 to 3 year term, and that the amount of the loan will be for $250,000.00. Mr. Lachona also stated that the Lender said that the commercial property would be the best property to borrow against, that the amount of the loan will address the issues of the loans the Trustee made and Ms. Sapone's needs, and that three years from now one of the properties will need to be sold. Mr. Siracusa stated it makes sense to get a hard money loan but that he would like to have something in writing from First Republic Bank stating that they will allow Ms. Sapone to a guarantor and that it is a huge difference in the interest rate. Mr. Mraule requested time to review the declaration and prepare a response. The Court expressed concern about ongoing financial issues and suggested a one week continuance to determine if First Republic will grant the loan. The Court also suggested an accounting period to commence January 1, 2014 if the loan is funded. Mr. Lachona stated that Judge Dearman waived accountings. Mr. Siracusa stated that he prefers the accountings be filed with the Court. The Petition for Order Authorizing Trustee to Borrow Money, etc. was continued to December 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Mraule is to file his response by Friday December 6, 2013. The Court suggested addressing the issue of the accounting period starting with January 1, 2014 through December 31, 2014 at the next hearing.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
